name: GT-jb-normsquare
source: HOS, refs/hos/joa-m.md:2314-2315 --- 3.1.4 JB algebra norm condition, VERBATIM: "A JB algebra is a Jordan Banach algebra A in which the norm satisfies the following two additional conditions for  $a, b \in A$ :" "(i)  $||a^2|| = ||a||^2$". B(H)_sa is a JB algebra (GT-bhsa-jc + GT-jc-is-jb), so ||a^2||=||a||^2 in operator norm for self-adjoint a, where a^2=aoa is the ambient Jordan square (=ordinary a^2 for self-adjoint a). Used for ||X^2||=||X||^2 norm bookkeeping.
